projects.description: Recent projects — websites, dashboards, and applications built for real businesses.

capabilities.heading: What I build
capabilities.description: End-to-end web development — from marketing sites to data-driven applications. Each capability includes design, development, and deployment.
capabilities.card1Desc: High-performance business sites, landing pages, and portfolio sites built for speed and conversions.
capabilities.card2Desc: Full-stack apps, SaaS frontends, and internal tools with real-time data and rich interactivity.
capabilities.card3Desc: Data visualization dashboards, admin panels, and reporting interfaces with live data integration.
capabilities.card4Desc: Custom storefronts, headless CMS integrations, and content-driven sites with seamless editing.

process.heading: How I build projects from brief to launch.
process.description: A compact step-by-step view of how each stage moves from alignment to live operations.
process.step1Summary: Understand goals, constraints, and expectations before writing code.
process.step1Detail: I start every project by understanding your business, users, and technical constraints. Through structured discovery sessions, we align on scope, priorities, and success metrics — creating a shared foundation before any design or development begins.
process.step2Summary: Architecture, content model, and component tree defined.
process.step2Detail: With goals clear, I map out the technical architecture, data model, and component hierarchy. This phase produces a detailed plan covering routes, states, performance budgets, and integration points — so there are no surprises mid-build.
process.step3Summary: Iterative development with continuous review checkpoints.
process.step3Detail: Development follows a structured cadence: core structure first, then interactions, then polish. Each milestone includes a review checkpoint where you see working software and can course-correct before the next cycle.
process.step4Summary: Performance audits and quality checks before launch.
process.step4Detail: Before going live, every page goes through Lighthouse audits, accessibility checks, responsive testing, and load testing. Issues are tracked and resolved against a shared checklist — no silent regressions.
process.step5Summary: Deploy, monitor, and iterate based on real usage.
process.step5Detail: Launch includes monitoring setup, error tracking, and analytics configuration. Post-launch, I review real usage data to identify optimization opportunities and plan the next iteration.

tech.heading: Performance & quality metrics
tech.description: I deliver production-ready code from day one. Each project follows a repeatable pipeline that prioritizes clarity, performance, and maintainability.
tech.timeline: Typical project delivery: 2–6 weeks depending on scope.
tech.bullet1: Built with modern standards — not page builders or templates.
tech.bullet2: Every project includes performance budgets and accessibility checks.
tech.bullet3: Clear timelines, regular updates, and no black-box development.
tech.bullet6: Source code delivered — no proprietary lock-in.

studio.metaDescription: How I work, what I value, and what you can expect when we build together. Operating principles, engagement models, and production standards.
studio.heading: How I work, what I value, and what you can expect.
studio.description: This page explains how I approach projects, the principles I follow, and what it's like to work together.
studio.principle1Implication: Every project starts with structured discovery — not assumptions.
studio.principle1Outcome: You get a clear scope with no hidden surprises.
studio.principle2Implication: Every component is built with performance budgets and measurable metrics.
studio.principle2Outcome: Sites ship fast and stay fast under real traffic.
studio.principle3Implication: You'll see progress in real time — no black boxes or silent delays.
studio.principle3Outcome: You're never left wondering where things stand.
studio.principle4Implication: Source code, design files, and documentation are delivered at the end.
studio.principle4Outcome: You can take your project anywhere — no lock-in.
studio.principle5Implication: Code is structured for maintenance, not just first launch.
studio.principle5Outcome: Updates and additions stay inexpensive over time.
studio.principle6Implication: Every feature is justified by user need, not trend.
studio.principle6Outcome: You get a focused product without unnecessary complexity.

services.heroDesc: Every project follows the same core process, adapted to your specific scope and timeline.
services.metaDescription: End-to-end web development process from discovery to deployment. Custom websites, dashboards, and tools — built to perform.
services.stage1Input: Your goals, brand guidelines, content outline
services.stage4Output: Lighthouse report, accessibility audit, final checks
services.model1Desc: Defined budget and timeline for well-understood projects. Best when requirements are clear from the start.
services.model2Desc: Ongoing development, maintenance, and iteration. Best for growing products that need continuous improvement.
services.model3Desc: Technical guidance, code review, and architecture planning. Best when you need expertise without full execution.
services.model4Desc: Quick turnaround for urgent fixes and critical issues. Best when something is broken and needs immediate attention.
services.service1Desc: Focused writing over real-time meetings. I respond within 24 hours on business days.
services.service2Desc: You work with me directly — no account managers, no handoffs. One point of contact end-to-end.
services.service3Desc: Work is structured into clear phases. You see progress at each stage, not just at the end.
services.service4Desc: Every project is built from the ground up. No theme customization, no pre-built wrappers.
services.example1Scope: Marketing site with content pages, contact flow, analytics setup
services.example2Scope: Data visualization, role-based views, API integration
services.example3Scope: Multi-tenant architecture, onboarding flow, subscription management
services.availabilityIntro: I'm a solo developer focused on delivering exceptional work for a small number of clients at a time.
services.availabilityNote1: I don't offer 24/7 support or emergency response. Communication is async-first during business hours.
services.availabilityNote2: I don't customize pre-built themes or templates. Every project is built from the ground up.
services.availabilityNote3: I'm selective about projects. If it's not a fit, I'll tell you honestly — and sometimes recommend someone else.
services.ctaSubtitle: Tell me about what you're building. I'll respond within 24 hours with a project brief.

contactPage.heading: Start a Project
contactPage.description: Tell me about your project and I'll get back to you within 24 hours.
contactPage.fitDesc: I work best with founders, agencies, and teams who value quality, transparency, and clear communication.
contactPage.fit1: You need a custom website or web application — not a template.
contactPage.fit2: You value performance, accessibility, and clean code.
contactPage.fit3: You want a partner who communicates clearly and consistently.
contactPage.fit4: You have a budget that supports quality work.
contactPage.step2: I'll review and respond within 24 hours
contactPage.step3: We align on scope, timeline, and next steps
contactPage.model1Desc: End-to-end delivery from strategy to launch. You provide the vision, I handle the execution.
contactPage.model2Desc: Start with a defined phase (e.g., design only) and extend as needed. Good for evolving requirements.
contactPage.model3Desc: Ongoing development and maintenance with a monthly commitment. Best for active products.
contactPage.model4Desc: Technical guidance, code review, and architecture planning without full execution.
contactPage.model5Desc: I work alongside your design team to handle development while you focus on the visual direction.
contactPage.model6Desc: Urgent fixes and rapid turnaround for critical issues. Best for production emergencies.
contactPage.faq1Question: What does a typical project cost?
contactPage.faq1Answer: It depends on scope. Small business sites typically fall in a lower range, custom dashboards in a mid range, and full platform builds at a higher tier. I'll provide a clear estimate during the project brief phase — no commitment needed.
contactPage.faq2Question: How long does a project take?
contactPage.faq2Answer: Most projects take 2–6 weeks depending on complexity. The timeline is defined during the brief phase after we've aligned on scope.
contactPage.faq3Question: What do you need from me to get started?
contactPage.faq3Answer: Clear goals, any existing brand materials or content, and a 30-minute call to align on scope. I handle the design, development, and deployment.
contactPage.faq4Answer: Yes. Every project includes a 30-day minor fixes window after launch. For ongoing work, I offer retainer arrangements tailored to your needs.
contactPage.faq5Question: What happens after I submit this form?
contactPage.faq5Answer: I read every inquiry personally within 24 hours. If it's a good fit, I'll respond with a project brief outlining scope, timeline, and next steps. No pressure — you decide whether to proceed.

audit.heading: Get a free website audit
audit.description: I'll review your current site and provide actionable recommendations to improve performance, SEO, and user experience.
audit.feature1Desc: Core Web Vitals, load times, and rendering bottlenecks identified and prioritized.
audit.feature2Desc: Meta tags, structured data, crawlability, and content gaps evaluated against best practices.
audit.feature3Desc: Navigation flow, mobile responsiveness, accessibility issues, and conversion path friction points.
audit.feature4Desc: Framework choices, hosting configuration, third-party dependencies, and security headers reviewed.
audit.successDesc: I'll review your site within 48 hours and send you a detailed report with recommendations.
audit.errorMessage: Something went wrong. Please email me directly at hello@jaostudio.dev.

cv.summary1: Frontend engineer focused on building fast, maintainable web experiences — from marketing sites to full-featured dashboards.

playground.heading: Playground
playground.description: Interactive demos are being rebuilt as production-grade components. Demonstrates range of motion, interaction, and design system capability.
playground.demo1Description: Card with mouse-driven 3D perspective transform. Hover to see the effect.
playground.demo2Description: Premium glass card effect with backdrop blur and subtle border glow.
playground.demo3Description: Elements that animate as they enter the viewport with staggered timing.
playground.demo4Description: Buttons that subtly follow the cursor position within their bounds.
playground.demo5Description: Multi-layer parallax scrolling that creates a sense of depth and immersion.
playground.demo6Description: Subtle hover and click feedback animations for UI elements.
playground.demo7Description: Premium pricing card comparison with animated tier highlighting.
playground.demo8Description: Smooth skeleton loading patterns and animated transitions.
